description_long: This is a tall, upright wooden easel, commonly used for holding canvases or drawing boards in place for painting or sketching. The structure features a robust H-frame design, providing stability with two vertical supports connected by horizontal crossbars and a wide, flat base. A central vertical post extends upward, with an adjustable mechanism visible for raising or lowering the canvas holder. The lower horizontal bar serves as a ledge to support the bottom of a canvas, while the upper clamp secures the top. The wood has a polished, dark brown finish, and the overall construction appears sturdy and functional, suitable for both indoor and outdoor urban art settings.
description: A tall, sturdy wooden H-frame easel with adjustable canvas holders and a polished dark brown finish. It features a wide base and robust vertical supports for stability.
description_view_0: Side view showing the slim profile, central vertical post, and the protruding canvas holder ledge.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the H-frame structure, two vertical supports, and the adjustable canvas holder bar.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the slim profile and showing the depth of the base and ledge.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the back of the H-frame, crossbars, and the central support post.
